description_long: This object is a rectangular stone block with a series of evenly spaced, parallel grooves running across its top surface, giving it a slatted appearance. The grooves are deep and dark, contrasting with the mottled, textured stone surface, which appears to be a composite of various earth tones such as brown, gray, and black. The block is solid and robust, with sharp, clean edges and a uniform thickness. Its sides are smooth and unadorned, while the top surface is the only area featuring the grooved pattern. The overall look suggests durability and functionality, likely intended to serve as a barrier or obstacle in an urban environment. The design is utilitarian, with no visible decorative elements or engravings.
description: A rectangular stone block with deep, parallel grooves on its top surface and a mottled, earth-toned texture. The block is solid, robust, and utilitarian in appearance.
description_view_0: Front view showing the full width of the block and the parallel grooves running horizontally across the top surface.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the depth and spacing of the grooves, with the grooves running vertically from this perspective.
description_view_2: Rear view similar to the front, displaying the full width and horizontal grooves on the top surface.
description_view_3: Opposite side view, again emphasizing the vertical alignment of the grooves and the block's thickness.
attribute_location_context: urban sidewalk or street drainage area
attribute_installation_method: embedded in concrete or set flush with pavement
attribute_weathering_pattern: No significant weathering is visible; the surface appears clean and well-maintained, with no moss, chips, or erosion.
attribute_integration_with_surrounding_s: flush alignment with pavement or adjacent blocks
